danger_signals.visual_form: Screen flash followed by transition to battle menu
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent danger; enemy engagement occurring on overworld

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct enemy character sprites (Kappa, Tiger Tank, masked ninja bandits)
danger_signals.meaning: Combat threat present; specific enemy type conveys threat level through design

danger_signals.visual_form: Tank vehicle with tiger rider sprite, added headlights, more threatening appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Major mechanical threat; redesigned from Japanese version to appear more dangerous

safety_signals.visual_form: Static town/settlement screens with NPCs and shops
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for interaction, item acquisition, quest progression; no random encounters

safety_signals.visual_form: Stationary character sprites in towns (Banko, quest-givers, merchants)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction available; information and support accessible

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Character sprite modifications between JP and US versions indicate cultural localization rather than faction identity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Tiger Tank enemy (Hittora in JP with swastika marking, redesigned in US)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy encounters distributed throughout overworld; invasion premise suggests widespread threat presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Normal town environments with NPCs; peaceful settlements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs provide dialogue, quests, and information about the invasion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overworld encounters with enemy sprites; threat-laden environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Random combat encounters triggered; player must fight or flee from enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jack and Ryu sprite improvements (added ninja gear details in US: headbands, sleeves, belt buckle, V-neck)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced character presentation reflects growing power and ninja identity; visual upgrade marks progression

environmental_storytelling.technique: Sprite design as character identity marker
environmental_storytelling.example: Jack and Ryu redesigned with ninja headbands, sleeves, and belt buckles in US version to communicate shifted cultural theme from kung-fu to ninja
Banko redesigned from male to female character (US version) with completely redrawn portrait, indicating narrative/thematic role adjustment
Kappa enemy redrawn for US version to appear goofier and less threatening (only head visible above water vs. arms visible in JP), communicating difficulty/danger level through visual design
Tiger Tank (Hittora) removed swastika and Hitler mustache in US version, restructuring enemy visual communication to remove politically sensitive iconography
Japanese version uses expanding 'X' screen transition during random encounters; US version removes this, changing how danger onset is visually communicated

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Facial expression changes to surprise when gong rings on title screen; communicates reactive personality
Throw arms up when cheering (US sprite redesign); visual celebration behavior added in localization
Trigger screen flash and transition to battle menu; behavioral manifestation of danger state

notes: Little Ninja Brothers demonstrates significant regional visual communication differences between Japanese (Super Chinese 2) and US releases. The core manifestation layer appears consistent (danger via encounters, safety via towns), but localization substantially altered faction identity markers. The ninja theme (headbands, outfit details) in US version vs. kung-fu in Japanese version represents deliberate world-state reframing. Enemy redesigns (Kappa, Tiger Tank) show how threat communication was culturally contextualized. The removal of the gong-reaction behavior and encounter 'X' transition in US version suggests deliberate pacing/presentation choices affecting how danger manifests to players. Character gender shifts (Banko) and censorship (Tiger Tank swastika) indicate content policy affecting visual faction/enemy communication.
